vientiane.title: Vientiane
vientiane.gettingThere: Fly to Wattay International Airport (VTE) — direct flights from Bangkok, Hanoi, Seoul, Kuala Lumpur, and more. Or cross the Thai-Lao Friendship Bridge from Nong Khai, Thailand.

essentials.gettingAround: Tuk-tuks within city (₭20,000–50,000). Bicycle rental ₭30,000/day. Scooter rental ₭80,000–120,000/day.

luang-prabang.title: Luang Prabang
luang-prabang.tagline: UNESCO World Heritage Jewel on the Mekong
luang-prabang.gettingThere: Fly to Luang Prabang International Airport (LPQ) from Bangkok, Hanoi, or Vientiane. Or take the Laos-China Railway from Vientiane (2 hours). Slow boat from Huay Xai (2 days) is a classic experience.

essentials.gettingAround: Walking for old town. Bicycle rental ₭30,000/day. Songthaew to Kuang Si (₭40,000 shared).
busConnections: Vientiane (10 hr bus or 2 hr train, ₭200,000 bus)
Vang Vieng (6 hr bus or 1.5 hr train)

vang-vieng.title: Vang Vieng
vang-vieng.tagline: Karst Peaks, Blue Lagoons, and River Adventures
vang-vieng.gettingThere: Take the Laos-China Railway from Vientiane (1 hour) or Luang Prabang (1.5 hours). Bus from Vientiane (4 hours, ₭80,000).
highlights: Blue Lagoon & Tham Phu Kham Cave

essentials.gettingAround: Bicycle rental ₭20,000/day. Scooter rental ₭80,000–100,000/day. Tuk-tuk for lagoons (₭30,000–50,000).
essentials.airport: No airport — access via Vientiane or Luang Prabang
busConnections: Vientiane (4 hr bus or 1 hr train, ₭80,000 bus)
Luang Prabang (6 hr bus or 1.5 hr train)
essentials.language: Lao, basic English in tourist areas

nong-khiaw.title: Nong Khiaw
nong-khiaw.tagline: Mountain Village on the Nam Ou River
nong-khiaw.gettingThere: Minivan from Luang Prabang (3 hours, ₭80,000). Boat from Muang Ngoi (1 hour upstream).
highlights: Muang Ngoi Day Trip by Boat

essentials.gettingAround: Walking for town. Bicycle rental ₭20,000/day. Boat to Muang Ngoi (₭50,000).
essentials.airport: No airport — access via Luang Prabang

phonsavan.title: Phonsavan
phonsavan.tagline: Plain of Jars — Ancient Mystery of Xieng Khouang
phonsavan.gettingThere: Fly to Xieng Khouang Airport (XKH) from Vientiane. Or bus from Vientiane (10 hours) or Luang Prabang (8 hours).

essentials.gettingAround: Hire a driver for jar sites (~₭300,000/day). Scooter rental ₭80,000/day. UXO warning: stay on marked paths.

thakhek.title: Thakhek
thakhek.tagline: The Loop — Caves, Karst, and Open Road
thakhek.gettingThere: Bus from Vientiane (6 hours, ₭120,000) or Pakse (6 hours). Cross from Nakhon Phanom, Thailand via the Third Friendship Bridge.

essentials.gettingAround: Scooter rental for The Loop (₭80,000–120,000/day). Bicycle for town.
essentials.airport: No airport — access via Vientiane or Savannakhet

pakse.title: Pakse
pakse.tagline: Gateway to Southern Laos and the Bolaven Plateau
pakse.gettingThere: Fly to Pakse International Airport (PKZ) from Vientiane, Bangkok, or Siem Reap. Bus from Vientiane (12 hours) or Thakhek (6 hours).

essentials.gettingAround: Scooter rental for Bolaven Plateau loop (₭80,000–120,000/day). Tuk-tuk in town (₭20,000–40,000).
essentials.language: Lao, some English in tourist areas

4000-islands.title: Si Phan Don (4000 Islands)
4000-islands.tagline: River Islands Where Time Stands Still
4000-islands.gettingThere: Bus from Pakse to Nakasang (3 hours, ₭60,000), then boat to Don Det (₭20,000) or Don Khon (₭25,000).
highlights: Khone Phapheng Falls (largest in SE Asia by volume)
Cycling Between Don Det and Don Khon

essentials.gettingAround: Bicycle rental ₭15,000/day. Walking between islands via bridge. Boat for dolphin watching (₭60,000).
essentials.language: Lao, basic English on tourist islands

bolaven-plateau.title: Bolaven Plateau
bolaven-plateau.gettingThere: Ride from Pakse on a scooter (the Bolaven Loop starts and ends in Pakse). Or arrange a guided tour from Pakse.

essentials.gettingAround: Scooter for the loop (₭80,000–120,000/day from Pakse). Small loop: 1 day. Big loop: 2-3 days.
essentials.language: Lao, tribal languages, very limited English

travelTips.currency: Lao Kip (LAK/₭). $1 USD ≈ ₭20,000. Thai Baht and USD widely accepted in tourist areas.
travelTips.visaFree: Most nationalities can get a 30-day visa on arrival ($30-42 depending on nationality). E-visa available for some countries.
travelTips.bestOverallMonths: November to March (cool dry season). October-November: lush green, fewer crowds. April: Pi Mai Lao (Lao New Year) — massive water festival.
travelTips.rainySeasonNote: May to September is rainy season. Roads can be difficult, especially unpaved routes. Burning season smoke (Feb-Apr) can reduce visibility in northern Laos.
travelTips.simCard: Unitel or Lao Telecom SIM at any airport or shop. ₭50,000-100,000 for 30 days data.
travelTips.transport: Laos-China Railway connects Vientiane, Vang Vieng, and Luang Prabang. Songthaews and tuk-tuks for local transport. Minivans for inter-city. Slow boat on the Mekong is a classic experience.
